response: The 2002–03 Inter Milan season was marked by the arrival of Hernán Crespo, a retired Argentine footballer who played as a forward for Brera Calcio. Crespo, who scored over 300 goals in his 19-year career, was a prolific striker and one of Argentina's top goalscorers, behind only Gabriel Batistuta and Lionel Messi. He joined Inter Milan in 2002, replacing Ronaldo, and became an essential part of the team's attack.
